title: Manufacturing Engineer
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:  

The New Product Introduction (NPI) Team is responsible for developing and scaling the assembly, integration, and test operations for our next generation of satellites. The manufacturing technicians and engineers on the team work together to establish the overall processes and work instructions for assembling and testing satellites from the component level through final spacecraft integration. As a member of the NPI team, you will collaborate closely with design engineers, test engineers, and other cross-functional teams to ensure seamless execution and delivery of spacecraft.

This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy hands-on work in building and testing complex products and take a methodical approach to manufacturing operations.You will regularly exercise discretion, and independent judgment, and act in a highly autonomous way. You will be expected to make decisions independent of oversight.

This is a full-time, in-office position based in our Berlin Manufacturing Hub 5 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Support the Assembly, Integration and Test of Satellites and subsystems
  • Develop/document assembly procedures and work instructions
  • Design mechanical and electrical equipment and fixtures to aid with assembly, integration, and testing
  • Drive non-conformances to resolution and establish actionable process improvements
  • Recommend facilities and capital equipment improvements for scaling our manufacturing processes to meet our production demand
  • Participate in training of production technicians to transition the manufacturing line from R&D to production
  • Provide DFM/DFA recommendations to design teams
  • Monitor production metrics, identify areas for improvement, and implement corrective actions

What You Bring:

  • 4+ years of relevant experience
  • Bachelors degree in Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Manufacturing engineering
  • Experience building satellites or similarly complex optical and electromechanical assemblies in a cleanroom environment
  • Experience reading technical drawings
  • Excellent SolidWorks skills to develop tooling & fixturing to interface with spacecraft and to extract useful views for use in work instructions
  • Hands-on experience with hardware assembly, integration, and test
  • Solid attention to detail and technical writing skills for documenting assembly and test processes

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience implementing successful continuous improvement processes
  • Experience with Manufacturing Execution System (MES) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software

About the Role:

The Media Programs Research Lead will join Planet’s Communications team to support the operations of the Media and Think Tank Program (MTTP). The MTTP works with reporters and subject matter experts at think tanks to use Planet’s images and products to report on disasters, environmental stories, geopolitical events, and other news of global interest. This person will collaborate internally with a broad range of Planet stakeholders, including Marketing, technical and NGO-focused teams, as well as externally with journalists and experts to provide insights into, and on top of, Planet data.

The ideal candidate is well-versed in geopolitics and has experience working with satellite data, is curious with an eye for compelling stories, and enjoys developing creative approaches to visualizing and explaining complex subjects.

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our San Francisco, CA or D.C. office in Arlington, VA 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Proactively identify and develop stories relevant to current events that showcase the value of Planet’s data and analytic products
  • Analyze satellite data as well as other earth observation data to document and explain events of general interest
  • Collaborate with Communications and Data Visualization teams on ideas for proactive storytelling and image packages to send to media and research customers and partners
  • Support rapid response efforts during crisis news events with image identification and analysis
  • Build and maintain relationships with reporters, analysts, and subject matter experts

What You Bring:

  • 4+ years experience working in a newsroom, think tank, or as a data analyst
  • Experience analyzing and interpreting satellite imagery
  • Experience with GIS tools and/or Adobe Photoshop
  • Experience supporting multiple projects simultaneously
  • Experience working collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders
  • Ability to work in a dynamic environment across multiple time zones
  • Excellent written and interpersonal communication skills; fluent in written and spoken English

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience working with open source investigative tools, datasets and methods
  • Ability and comfort communicating results to external audiences
  • Relationships within think tank and press communities

title: Opto-Mechanical Engineer
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

The Electro-Optical Systems team at Planet is seeking an Opto-Mechanical Engineer to help drive the design of payloads in Planet’s fleet of Earth-imaging satellites. Your mission will be to champion our payload design efforts, with a specific focus on opto-mechanical design and manufacturing. The ideal candidate will be a seasoned opto-mechanical designer who has led optical system engineering efforts across multiple programs from conceptual design through flight. As a member of the Electro-Optical Systems team within Planet’s Space Systems division, you will be a key contributor and stakeholder in Planet’s agile aerospace approach to spacecraft design, and will work hand-in-glove with optical engineering, structural analysis, thermal engineering,  and reliability & qualification, as well as the rest of Planet’s leading-edge spacecraft design and manufacturing teams. 

This is a full-time, in-office position based in our San Francisco HQ 5 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Own the opto-mechanical manufacturing process of optical payloads on Planet’s next-generation satellites
  • Design the manufacturing line for mid-volume production of optical payloads. Make first principle based, data-driven decisions throughout the design process to ensure efficient assembly and test processes
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams such as structural, thermal analysts and optical engineer groups to drive and perform analyses to ensure payloads meet stiffness, strength, thermal, and launch survivability requirements, as well as maintain on-orbit performance through STOP (Structural, Thermal, Optical Performance)
  • Drive part and assembly design, manage fabrication with key vendors, perform failure analysis, and execute tolerance stackup and GD&T analyses to ensure precision and reliability
  • Develop and maintain comprehensive design documentation 
  • Lead technical presentations and reviews across all design review gates
  • Support test planning and qualification test campaigns
  • Be hands-on with flight and test hardware

What You Bring:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related technical field.
  • 4+ years experience owning the development of opto-mechanical products from requirements through conceptual design, testing and manufacturing
  • Experience designing for high-reliability space environments including launch survivability, vacuum conditions, on-orbit performance through thermal conditions (STOP)
  • Experience in the design and fabrication of optical assemblies, incorporating components made from glass, carbon fiber, metals, ceramics, epoxies, as well as mechanisms and electrical elements
  • Practical and direct engagement with hardware, from prototyping to production and testing phases
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Experience working in a dynamic environment while driving projects to completion
  • Good verbal and visual communication skills

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Expertise in optics / opto-mechanical manufacturing, metrology, alignment and calibration
  • Ability to build out the manufacturing line for precision optical systems
  • Experience with dynamic hardware builds, vibration and shock testing, telescope assembly, and optical performance testing
  • Experience applying systems engineering principles to optimize optical payload integration, focusing on requirements management, trade-off analysis, and balancing system-level constraints such as cost, schedule, and performance
  • Ability to drive manufacturers and DFM/DFA
  • Proficiency in SolidWorks, Zemax, Matlab, Visual Basic, Python and/or C/C++

You will act as a pivotal cross-functional collaborator, working alongside Sales, Pre-sales, Customer Success, and Business Development to integrate partner solutions into large-scale customer contracts. This position is ideal for a professional who thrives at the intersection of high-growth technology and national security, ensuring our partners are equipped to deliver Planet’s high-frequency imagery to those who need it most.

  • Coordinate cross-functionally with Sales and Pre-sales to support partner-related components of key government tenders and contracts. 
  • Partner daily with Customer Success and Business Development teams to ensure seamless delivery of Planet solutions enabled by partnerships. 
  • Uphold Planet’s mission by enabling transparent, timely geospatial data to support global security and humanitarian efforts.

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.
  • Background in Partnership Management or Business Development, specifically managing complex, multi-stakeholder alliances. 
  • Ability to navigate the EMEA market, including an understanding of regional regulatory or sovereign requirements.

  • A collaborative mindset to work effectively across globally distributed, cross-functional teams.

title: PreSales Sr. Solutions Architect - French Defense and Intelligence
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role: 

As a Pre-Sales Solutions Architect, you'll demonstrate the value of Planet's data and services to help organizations within the Western and Northern European regions overcome their earth data challenges. You'll also collaborate with internal cross-functional teams, including Customer Success, Product Management, and Business Development, to act as the voice of the customer to enhance Planet's industry offerings.

The ideal candidate is passionate about Earth observation and enjoys helping customers get most out of their work. This role will appeal to candidates who don’t mind frequent international travel (primarily around Europe) and are comfortable working in the defense and intelligence sector.

This is a full-time, remote position based in France or the United Kingdom. This role requires frequent international travel up to 50%. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Build and deliver presentations and product demonstrations highlighting technical capabilities of Planet’s platform and our ability to fulfill customer’s business needs.
  • Create relationships with customers’ technical and management staff as a trusted advisor to their business.
  • Act as an advisor to the customer to recommend a solutions architecture that fits their business challenges.
  • Assist with the response to tenders, RFI’s and RFP’s proposals with respect to product/solution information.
  • Work with your sales team colleagues to determine account development plans and execute a strategy to engage customers and prospects.
  • Collaborate with internal cross-functional teams including Customer Success, Product Management, and Business Development to act as the voice of the customer, assist with RFP and RFI preparation and submissions.
  • Attain quarterly and annual sales and operational objectives assigned by management.
  • Represent Planet’s Defense and Intelligence needs to Sales leadership, in communicating forecasts, pipeline opportunities, deal reviews, data in CRM tools, and customer feedback.
  • Present to key executive Government stakeholders within the Western and Northern Europe Regions.
  • Act as a mentor to team members and lead training to drive their development.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience in supporting the Defense community within the European region.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a related field.
  • Have the ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance for meeting with regional customers.
  • Ability to travel internationally up to 50%, primarily around Europe and the United States.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in French.
  • Professional working proficiency in English, the language of the company.
  • Experience working with GIS and Remote Sensing users within the Defense industry.
  • Experience in IMINT, ISR, and related fields.
  • Experience with QGIS, SocetGXP, ArcGIS, GDAL and other common geospatial software packages.
  • Ability to successfully communicate and coordinate with international customers & sales team.
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and proactive working style.
  • Ability to multitask, prioritize and manage time effectively.
  • Ability to program with Python to process imagery, manipulate vector data, interact with APIs and build data pipelines.
  • Ability to lead customer engagements.
  • Ability to run workshops or training sessions with users.

Application Deadline:

May 18, 2026 by 11:59p / 23:59 CET (Central European Time)

Export Control Requirements:

Planet Labs Germany GmbH is authorized to receive U.S. export-controlled information at Planet under an export license issued by the Bureau of Industry and Security. This position requires access to U.S. and German/EU export-controlled information, and as such, employment (or hiring of a contractor) is contingent upon the candidate’s ability to access all applicable export-controlled information without additional export licensing being required by German, EU, or U.S. authorities.

title: Product Manager, Tanager
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

 

About the Role: 

As a Product Manager for Tanager at Planet, you will be responsible for overseeing product development and setting product strategy for our hyperspectral data products. Planet’s Tanager-1 satellite is operational, and its data products have achieved General Availability. You will now oversee the product's continued evolution and scaling to ensure the hyperspectral offering delivers valuable and actionable insights for end users in commercial, government, and non-profit domains. You will define the product roadmap in alignment with overall company strategy and engage customers to understand needs and set product requirements to meet them. You will work very cross-functional with Sales, Marketing, Science, Software Engineering, Legal, and other teams at Planet. At its core, this role requires strong product leadership including listening deeply to customers, mobilizing cross-functional teams, and making clear, accountable decisions to drive outcomes in ambiguous environments. The Tanager Mission  is at the core of Planet's commitment to "Do Good" through the monitoring of global emissions and our impactful partnership with Carbon Mapper.

Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Impact You'll Own:

  1. Oversee the evolution and scaling of our hyperspectral products, setting clear strategy and making principled tradeoffs across competing priorities, constraints, and uncertainty to drive revenue growth as the constellation scales. 
  2. Drive commercial growth by growing the foothold in the methane market and strategically evolving the product portfolio into new markets to find product-market fit.
  3. Collaborate with Platform Product teams to ensure customers can task, order, and access our products
  4. Build and maintain the Product Roadmap for our Hyperspectral Offering, synthesizing inputs from customers and internal stakeholders (Sales, Engineering, Customer Success, etc.) while making clear, accountable prioritization decisions aligned with company strategy. 
  5. Work closely with Sales and Product Marketing to clearly articulate value of the hyperspectral derived products for targeted markets
  6. Lead cross-functional alignment across Engineering, Science, and Commercial teams, ensuring clarity of direction while navigating ambiguity and evolving constraints.
  7. Collaborate with Solutions teams to explore how Tanager data can enhance Planet's internal products

What You Bring:

  1. 5+ years as a power-user of satellite imagery, remote sensing and GIS, preferably with experience using hyperspectral imagery
  2. Demonstrated ability to operate in ambiguity, assess risk, and make high-quality decisions with incomplete data, with strong product judgment and a proven track record of making difficult tradeoffs without relying on consensus.
  3. Experience in an industry where advanced sensing could be applied such as defense, atmospheric sciences, agriculture, energy, environmental conservation or geology 
  4. A solid understanding of the benefits and challenges of processing and analyzing EO data 
  5. Proven capacity for delighting customers
  6. Experience working in a fast paced, high growth environment
  7. Outstanding executive communication (speaking and writing), interpersonal, and organizational skills
  8. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and creative thinking skills
  9. Ability to collaborate well across a wide variety of stakeholders

 

#LI-REMOTE

July 27, 2026 by 11:59p / 23:59 CET (Central European Time)

description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:  

Planet's hiring engine moves only as fast as our ability to give every candidate a clear, organized, and respectful experience from first conversation to first day. As Recruiting Coordinator, you will be the operational anchor of that experience — owning interview scheduling, candidate communication, and process logistics across Planet's global hiring portfolio. This role sits at the intersection of nearly every team at Planet: hiring managers, Talent Acquisition Partners, executive assistants, candidates, and the broader People team.

This is a full-time role based in our Washington, DC office, reporting to the Senior Manager, Talent Operations

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Schedule interviews of all types — phone screens, full panels, executive debriefs — across multiple time zones, calendars, and interviewer preferences.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for candidates throughout the interview process, ensuring every touchpoint feels organized, welcoming, and on time.
  • Maintain accurate, complete, and audit-ready data in Greenhouse — including candidate stages, scorecard status, interviewer assignments, and offer details.
  • Partner with Talent Acquisition Partners to keep candidate pipelines moving — flagging blockers, escalating delays, and proactively troubleshooting scheduling friction.
  • Support continuous improvement of the recruiting coordination workflow — proposing better templates, calendar conventions, and process documentation that scale across the team.
  • Contribute to candidate experience initiatives, including survey follow-up, debrief discipline, and process feedback loops that close the gap between intent and execution.

What You Bring:

  • 1+ year of professional experience in coordination, scheduling, customer service, operations, or a comparable role that required juggling multiple stakeholders and high attention to detail.
  • Meticulous attention to detail — calendar invites are right the first time, candidate names are spelled correctly, and links work on the first click.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication, with a warm, professional tone in candidate-facing email and chat.
  • Comfort with calendaring tools (Google Calendar), video conferencing (Zoom or Google Meet), and a willingness to quickly learn modern recruiting tooling such as Greenhouse and GoodTime.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and adjust quickly when schedules shift, candidates re-route, or hiring needs change.
  • Genuine customer service orientation — you treat every candidate, hiring manager, and interviewer like the most important person in your inbox.
  • Self-directed organization — you build your own structure for managing a high-volume workload without needing daily supervision.

What Makes You Stand Out: 

  • Prior recruiting coordination, talent operations, or executive assistant experience.
  • Hands-on experience with Greenhouse, GoodTime, or other modern recruiting and scheduling platforms.
  • Experience supporting a team that operates across multiple time zones, including U.S. East, U.S. West, and Europe.
  • Background in project management, process improvement, or operational excellence.
  • Interest in a long-term career in People, Talent, or HR — with curiosity about how recruiting operations scales as a company grows.

Application Deadline: August 19, 2026 by 11:59p / 23:59 CET (Central European Time)

In this role, you will also act as a bridge between current sales operations and future business opportunities, investigating unverified use cases and testing market viability before they reach the broader sales team.

As an integral part of the sales organization, the SDR will work closely with the Sales and broader GTM Strategy team to develop a qualified and closable pipeline. You will be responsible for strategic outreach, account research, and multi-channel engagement to connect with potential customers and position our solutions effectively.

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who can identify a customer’s core needs and develop conceptual frameworks or early-stage demonstrations. You will be responsible for determining which opportunities have the potential for scale and which require different strategic pathways, such as partner-led motions or automation.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Facilitate deep-dive discovery in verified and unverified markets to identify high-impact opportunities 
  • Conduct initial research and outreach to define customer challenges and determine where our solutions provide the most significant value and execute strategic outreach through personalized emails, cold calls, social selling, and industry events.
  • Leverage modern digital tools and AI-assisted workflows to create mock-ups, demonstrations, or "proof-of-concept" applications that illustrate potential solutions for prospects.
  • Analyze early-stage data to determine the potential scale of new segments and identify the most effective sales or partnership channels.

  • Ability to work independently in a dynamic, target-driven environment.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Environmental Science, or other relevant major

  • Experience in a consultative sales approach, effectively leading early-stage sales conversations and uncovering deeper business needs.
  • Ability to quickly learn and articulate complex solutions, particularly in geospatial data, SaaS, or analytics-driven industries.

description_text:

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

The Role

Planet is seeking a Business Development Manager focused on the Asia, Pacific, Japan region and their government’s National Security Communities to lead execution of the entire business development lifecycle, with a specific focus on Australia. Responsibilities include identifying and qualifying new business opportunities, pursuing long term strategic captures and supporting acquisition activities such as RFI’s, White Papers, Unsolicited Proposals, and RFPs across the region's D&I portfolio, while advocating for Planet capabilities to mission users within national Defense and Intelligence organizations. The BD Manager will contribute to the identiﬁcation, prioritization and shaping of new business pursuits and the development and execution of capture strategies focusing on the Australian Defense Forces, Defense Intelligence Group, and Joint Operations Command. Future pursuits may expand across the entirety of the associated region, to include  Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Vietnam, New Zealand, and others.

The ideal candidate knows how to navigate the complex relationships of a dynamic technology company with multiple priorities and will inspire our team and our partners.  Planet’s services are novel, and we are looking for someone to educate stakeholders on the broader context of opportunities and keeping an eye on building the case for new product development.

This is a full-time, remote position based in Australia. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Leverage existing and develop new, trusted relationships with current and prospective Planet customers and industry partners
  • Specifically, the role will need to create a strategy and engagement plan to align Planet solutions to increase support for Australia’s National Security mission needs
  • Actively participate in relevant community forums, events, industry days, and other means to continually increase Planet’s understanding of the Customer’s missions, long term visions and priorities, along with budgets and acquisition cycles to position Planet to earn new business and partnership opportunities
  • Build the long term pipeline at Defense and Intelligence organizations within the Australian National Security Community:
    • Identify Mechanisms to Influence and Capture Future Business (e.g. RFIs, RFPs, White Papers)
    • Adjudicate bid/no-bid or pursue/don’t-pursue evaluation, documentation, recommendation, consensus and way forward in coordination with Sales teams
    • Establish repeatable business development processes
    • Determine resource needs outside of current (e.g. consultants, subcontractors), justify resource needs, budgets and contracts, and manage the above
    • Document and continuously update Customer engagement plans and key messaging in partnership with Global D&I Director of Business Development
  • Provide leadership and expertise in support of strategic planning, competitive intelligence and market assessments
  • Partner collaboratively with APJ Sales Director and Account Executives
  • Maintain close alignment with Government Affairs on policy shaping activities
  • Maintain close relationships with BD Managers across regions, to ensure consistent approaches where appropriate and coordinate engagements with multi-country Defense alliances that cross regions
  • Coordinate responses to RFIs and RFPs through bid decision to submission to ensure alignment to strategy
  • Collaborate with Proposal Managers and Product Leadership on product positioning and pricing

What You Bring:

  • 10+ years of relevant experience
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field
  • Experience working within the Australian National Security Community (e.g. Australian Defence Forces, Defence Intelligence Group, Joint Operations Command, Home Affairs) as a civilian or military officer
  • Ability and experience in communicating effectively with senior level civilian leadership and/or military flag officers
  • Experience forging mutually beneficial relationships with Defence and Intelligence organizations
  • Excellent communication (speaking and writing), presentation, interpersonal, and organizational skills
  • Achievements and relevant experience in the private and/or public sector in some combination of aerospace and remote sensing, data analytics, sales, business development, and military and intelligence roles
  • Excellent project, time, and budget management skills
  • Experience working collaboratively and independently in a dynamic environment with the ability to tackle unique challenges presented by its dynamic conditions
  • Experience working closely with organizations in a wide variety of geographies and sectors
  • Highly motivated to create a vibrant 21st century aerospace ecosystem and a commercial GEOINT market

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience working across multiple parts of Australia’s National Security Community, preferably in GEOINT, Data, Space, Intelligence, Maritime functions in operational or strategic roles
  • Active security clearance

title: Senior Camera Hardware Engineer
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

Planet seeks a Senior Camera Hardware Engineer to serve as the lead electrical architect for our satellite imaging payloads within the Electrical Engineering team. This role encompasses the complete electrical life cycle of our camera systems, including recommending the optimal image sensor, designing ultra-low-noise power rails, and guaranteeing high-speed data integrity from the sensor to the FPGA. The engineer will collaborate closely with Electrical, Mechanical, Thermal and Optical engineers to optimize the entire optical payload (camera and telescope) to meet our mission requirements.

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our San Francisco office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Design and own the high-speed PCBA architectures for next-generation camera payloads, including sensor headboards and data acquisition systems.
  • Collaborate with Payload Systems Engineers to define the electrical specification and selection of CMOS image sensors, focusing on quantum efficiency, full-well capacity, and minimizing temporal/spatial noise.
  • Architect low-noise power delivery networks (PDN) and high-speed differential signaling (LVDS/MIPI) to ensure pristine image data capture.
  • Partner with Firmware/FPGA teams to optimize the Image Signal Processing pipeline, implementing hardware-level calibration for dark current and defective pixels.
  • Drive the electrical requirements for precision sensor alignment, including the design of flexible interconnects and thermal monitoring circuits that maintain focus across orbital temperature swings.

What You Bring:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or Mechanical Engineering or a closely related field.
  • 10+ years of experience in hardware design, with a significant focus on digital/analog imaging systems and camera hardware.
  • Expertise in CMOS sensor operation, including the ability to model and design practical solutions to minimize electronic noise.
  • Hands-on experience with high-end oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and optical test equipment (monochromators, integrating spheres).
  • Ability to navigate the trade-offs between power consumption, thermal dissipation, and image SNR.

What Makes You Stand Out: 

  • Experience with Earth observation payload specifications and remote sensing mission requirements.
  • Experience designing electronics for the LEO environment, including knowledge of radiation mitigation and vacuum-compatible design.
  • Professional experience with FPGA-based signal processing and high-speed data serialization.
  • Deep experience in precision analog design specifically for sensor biasing and high-resolution ADC interfacing.

Application Deadline:

title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role: 

We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer to help develop transformative solutions for our most strategic accounts, starting with our Global Defense and Security customers. In this role, you will work directly with key partners, acting as a senior technical contributor and hands-on builder within a collaborative engineering team. Your mission will be to tackle complex, mission-critical customer challenges by building high-impact software solutions.

You will have a high degree of autonomy to design and build what is needed, turning ambiguous requirements into functional and reliable software. Your work will directly contribute to delivering on critical customer commitments and will help inform future product development. This role is ideal for an individual contributor who thrives in dynamic environments and enjoys taking ownership of complex technical problems from design to delivery.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the Netherlands or Belgium with up to 50% time onsite with the customer. If located near the office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Contribute to the full development lifecycle: from discovery and decomposition to designing, building, and deploying bespoke solutions for customer challenges.
  • Design and develop robust, full-stack software, including front-end, back-end, data integration, cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS), and CI/CD pipelines.
  • Work directly with customers to understand their operational challenges and translate them into clear technical requirements.
  • Identify reusable patterns in customer solutions to inform broader platform development.
  • Leverage the latest AI tooling and an AI-first mindset to drive rapid iteration, deliver value quickly, and adapt to evolving requirements with a bias for action.
  • Mentor other engineers, contribute to code reviews, and help foster a collaborative team environment.
  • Deliver critical commitments on time, understanding their direct impact on customer relationships and business objectives.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of relevant work experience in software engineering or in an equivalent technical role.
  • Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant field.
  • Experience with geospatial software development and concepts (e.g., Rasterio, Fiona, Shapely, OpenLayers, GDAL).
  • AWS or GCP experience.
  • Proficiency in Python.
  • Excellent knowledge of software engineering best practices, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, and testing methodologies.
  • Excellent customer engagement skills, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.
  • Experience with fast, iterative development cycles and an ability to adapt to shifting requirements.
  • Willingness to travel 30-50% to customer sites within the region.
  • Have the ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance.

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience with JavaScript, Java, Go or C#

title: Senior Manager, Talent Operations
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

Planet is executing one of its most ambitious hiring years — onboarding hundreds of new team members across multiple geographies to deliver on our growth plan, launch a second manufacturing hub in Berlin, and scale our Earth intelligence products to market. Behind every hire is a complex operational machine: ATS workflows, interview scheduling, offer approvals, compliance checks, onboarding handoffs, and dozens of process touchpoints that determine whether a candidate has a world-class experience or drops out of the funnel.

As Senior Manager, Talent Operations, you will own that machine. You will lead a team of operations specialists, recruiting coordinators, and support staff that keeps the entire hiring lifecycle running smoothly — from requisition creation through offer acceptance. You will drive the Talent Operations Roadmap, which includes active projects spanning AI interview assessment tool deployment, global process standardization, headcount approval flow redesign, SLA audits, and interview process documentation.

This role sits at the intersection of process design, technology, compliance, and candidate experience. You will be responsible for ensuring that our operational infrastructure scales at the same pace as our hiring ambitions — and that every touchpoint in the recruiting process reflects the quality and rigor candidates expect from a company building technology that monitors the entire planet.

This role reports directly to the Senior Director of Talent Acquisition and Talent Management. You will partner closely with the Senior Manager, Talent Acquisition to ensure that the operational data flowing out of your systems — pipeline velocity, stage conversion rates, scheduling throughput — feeds directly into the capacity model that drives recruiting resource allocation. This is a critical role. This hire will immediately unblock a significant body of work and drive operational leadership to a function that touches every hire the company makes.

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our San Francisco office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Own and execute the Talent Operations Roadmap — a portfolio of active projects including AI assessment tool evaluation and rollout, global process standardization, headcount approval flow redesign, SLA audit and enforcement, and interview training program development.
  • Lead and develop a team of operations and coordination professionals across multiple offices, setting priorities, coaching on operational excellence, and ensuring coordination coverage meets the needs of a growing recruiting team.
  • Drive scorecard SLA compliance to target by implementing process controls, automated reminders, and accountability mechanisms within the ATS — turning a lagging metric into a leading indicator of interview quality.
  • Reduce time-to-fill by identifying and streamlining operational bottlenecks — particularly in interview scheduling, offer approval workflows, and pre-offer document processing.
  • Operationalize the selected AI interview assessment tool across the full recruiting team, including training, change management, hiring manager onboarding, and usage analytics tracking.
  • Own ATS administration, configuration, and optimization — including custom workflows, approval chains, reporting dashboards, offer templates, and compliance-driven fields for global hiring across multiple entities and geographies.
  • Lead the headcount approval flow redesign — documenting the current state, mapping the ideal state, building automated workflows, and launching with clear communication and documentation.
  • Ensure onboarding compliance for all new hires across multiple geographies, working closely with People Operations to hand off candidates cleanly and verify all pre-hire documentation including background checks, export control clearances, work authorization, and international entity requirements.
  • Build and maintain the talent operations knowledge base — ensuring all processes, policies, and SLAs are documented, current, and accessible to the recruiting team and hiring managers.
  • Partner with the Senior Manager, Talent Acquisition to provide the operational data that feeds the capacity model — including pipeline velocity metrics, stage conversion rates, and scheduling throughput — so that resource allocation decisions are grounded in real process performance.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience in talent operations, or HR operations.
  • 4+ years managing a team of coordinators and operations specialists at a scaling technology company.
  • Deep expertise with Greenhouse ATS or a comparable enterprise applicant tracking system, including administration, workflow configuration, custom reporting, and integration management.
  • Experience driving SLA compliance and process adherence across a distributed recruiting team — you have built the dashboards, set the targets, and held people accountable.
  • Experience managing recruiting coordination at scale (100+ hires per quarter), including complex multi-timezone scheduling, onsite coordination, and high-volume offer processing.
  • Track record of successfully deploying new recruiting technology — AI tools, scheduling automation, assessment platforms — with measurable adoption and impact metrics.
  • Project management capability to run multiple concurrent workstreams (roadmap execution, tool deployment, process redesign) without dropping priorities.
  • Experience with global hiring operations, particularly EMEA compliance, offer letter localization, and navigating local employment requirements.
  • Data-oriented mindset — you use pipeline metrics, SLA data, and process analytics to identify bottlenecks and make decisions, not intuition alone.

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience supporting a German entity setup, including familiarity with German employment compliance.
  • Background in aerospace, hardware, or manufacturing recruiting operations where compliance requirements such as export control and security clearances add operational complexity.
  • Experience with headcount planning and approval workflow tools that integrate with HRIS and finance systems.
  • Lean Six Sigma, PMP, or similar process improvement credentials that demonstrate operational rigor.
  • Experience building or rebuilding a talent operations function from the ground up — including establishing SLAs, standing up reporting, and creating documentation where little or none existed.

As a member of the Manufacturing Test Engineering team, you will collaborate with design engineers to develop board-level tests and be responsible for designing and developing the test fixtures, test hardware, and test software. This is a full-time, in-office position based in our Berlin Manufacturing Center 5 days a week.

  • Support manufacturing operations with response to test station issues
  • Maintain and improve existing manufacturing test stations
  • Drive the strategy and approach to PCBA test for manufacturing
  • Oversee test software deployment and provisioning
  • Update and manage existing test pass/fail requirements
  • Continuous improvement of test software and hardware to ensure high reliability and scalability of test systems
  • Troubleshoot station failures and debug PCBA non-conformances through all stages of test, drive root cause analysis, and recommend corrective actions

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience in PCBA testing or manufacturing electrical test development and sustainment
  • Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Computer, Aerospace or similar engineering discipline
  • Ability to read and interpret PCBA schematics and use them to understand functionality and troubleshoot hardware
  • Experience with JTAG/boundary scan, bed-of-nail testers, and functional tests
  • Experience developing and documenting operating procedures and guides
  • Experience troubleshooting hardware/software issues and identifying root causes
  • Experience with measurement hardware and test equipment such as DMMs, oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, analog and digital I/O, data acquisition systems, switch matrices, relay banks, and signal conditioning
  • Experience programming in Python, especially functional tests of hardware
  • Experience managing multiple Linux systems in a test environment
  • Experience with DVCS such as git or mercurial

  • Advanced Python programming skills with a track record of developing and contributing to a large and complex code base
  • Experience in design, manufacturing, or test of satellite or aerospace hardware
  • Experience writing drivers for test hardware and peripherals
  • Extensive experience on the floor supporting and maintaining an active production line
  • Experience setting up trends and analysis on test data and actioning on those insights to improve test coverage and yield

description_text:

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

We are looking for a Senior Product Designer to design and launch geospatial web applications that transform satellite data into mission-critical intelligence. You will help build our next-generation monitoring platform, designed to provide near real-time observability of any location on Earth.

While our foundation includes environmental and agricultural monitoring, we are rapidly expanding our core capabilities to support Defense & Intelligence (D&I) and Global Monitoring Services.  You will play a key role in evolving this platform into a scalable system built for both tactical decision-making and global-scale analytics.

You bring a track record of designing sophisticated, data-heavy geospatial applications, ideally featuring AI-powered tools or complex enterprise data visualization. You will help shape a frontend architecture that remains performant and intuitive for everything from regional security assessments to global resource management.

This is a full-time, hybrid role requiring 3 days per week in our Berlin, Haarlem, Graz or Ljubljana offices.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Product Design Ownership: Lead the end-to-end process from discovery and prototyping to high-fidelity design.
  • GIS Interface Design: Create intuitive, powerful geospatial interfaces that translate complex data into actionable insights.
  • User Research: Gather feedback and conduct user interviews in close collaboration with product partners.
  • UI Architecture: Define and architect the interaction patterns and design system for Planet’s monitoring platform.
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with product and engineering to ensure top-tier design quality.
  • Culture: Help build and elevate Planet's design culture.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience designing complex digital products, especially bringing new technical, SaaS, or B2B products to market.
  • Bachelor’s degree in design or an equivalent field.
  • End-to-end experience: taking projects from early ideation to live software, and iterating based on feedback.
  • Proficiency in Figma and enterprise design systems.
  • A solid portfolio demonstrating complex professional projects, your process, and the resulting impact.
  • Experience conducting lightweight user research and usability testing, synthesizing findings into actionable improvements.
  • A solid core skillset: UX/UI, interaction design, rapid prototyping, accessibility, and visual design principles.
  • Ability to work quickly in undefined and ambiguous environments

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Advanced experience with GIS, geospatial data, or remote sensing. 
  • An AI-native mindset, utilizing AI tools for creative ideation, design, or generation.
  • A strong track record of taking ownership to communicate and solve complex, systemic problems.
  • Deep empathy for both end-users and internal stakeholders, expertly balancing usability with business needs.
  • Experience thriving in growth-focused, highly collaborative team environments.

description_text:

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

We are looking for a Senior Product Designer to design and launch geospatial web applications that transform satellite data into mission-critical intelligence. You will help build our next-generation monitoring platform, designed to provide near real-time observability of any location on Earth.

While our foundation includes environmental and agricultural monitoring, we are rapidly expanding our core capabilities to support Defense & Intelligence (D&I) and Global Monitoring Services.  You will play a key role in evolving this platform into a scalable system built for both tactical decision-making and global-scale analytics.

You bring a track record of designing sophisticated, data-heavy geospatial applications, ideally featuring AI-powered tools or complex enterprise data visualization. You will help shape a frontend architecture that remains performant and intuitive for everything from regional security assessments to global resource management.

This is a full-time, hybrid role requiring 3 days per week in our Berlin, Haarlem, Graz or Ljubljana offices.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Product Design Ownership: Lead the end-to-end process from discovery and prototyping to high-fidelity design.

  • UI Architecture: Define and architect the interaction patterns and design system for Planet’s monitoring platform.
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with product and engineering to ensure top-tier design quality.
  • Culture: Help build and elevate Planet's design culture.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience designing complex digital products, especially bringing new technical, SaaS, or B2B products to market.
  • Bachelor’s degree in design or an equivalent field.
  • End-to-end experience: taking projects from early ideation to live software, and iterating based on feedback.
  • Proficiency in Figma and enterprise design systems.
  • A solid portfolio demonstrating complex professional projects, your process, and the resulting impact.
  • Experience conducting lightweight user research and usability testing, synthesizing findings into actionable improvements.
  • A solid core skillset: UX/UI, interaction design, rapid prototyping, accessibility, and visual design principles.

  • Advanced experience with GIS, geospatial data, or remote sensing.

  • A strong track record of taking ownership to communicate and solve complex, systemic problems.
  • Deep empathy for both end-users and internal stakeholders, expertly balancing usability with business needs.

description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

As a Product Manager for Real-Time Insights at Planet, you will lead the charge in transforming how the world interacts with Earth observation data. You will be responsible for a cutting-edge, multi-mission capability designed to collapse the time between "observation" and "action." By leveraging technologies like on-satellite edge computing, rapid tasking, and ultra-low latency downlink, you will enable customers to make critical decisions in minutes, not days.

You will own the product lifecycle from concept to launch, navigating the technical trade-offs of space-based processing while ensuring a seamless experience for users in high-stakes environments like emergency response and national defense. This role is central to Planet’s mission of making global change visible, accessible and actionable, specifically where every second counts. 

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our San Francisco office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Define and drive the product vision for Real-Time Insights, prioritizing capabilities like ship/plane identification, cloud avoidance and change detection at the edge.
  • Develop go-to-market strategies and pricing models for premium, low-latency data tiers in coordination with Sales and Finance.
  • High velocity development of near real-time analysis, collaborating with AI teams, Space Systems and Ground Systems to optimize "pixel-to-insight" latency.
  • Gather and prioritize requirements for near real time use cases, ensuring our products meet the needs of defense, security and first-responder customers.
  • Serve as the product and market expert for Real-Time Insights, representing Planet in technical forums and high-level customer briefings.
  • Work with compliance and security teams to ensure all products and services adhere to export control laws, national security constraints, and data privacy standards.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of Product Management experience, specifically with a track record of launching complex, technical products.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.
  • Understanding of satellite systems,  tasking conops, edge compute and/or data processing.
  • Ability to understand and represent the needs of users in mission-critical, near real time environments such as defense, intelligence, or disaster response.
  • Experience balancing technical trade-offs and customer needs in complex product ecosystems.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.
  • Exceptional communication skills, with a knack for explaining complex technical trade-offs to both executive and engineering audiences.
  • Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously and prioritize appropriately.
  • Eligibility for US Export Control licenses (ITAR/EAR), as you will be working with sensitive satellite technologies.

What Makes You Stand Out: 

  • Hands-on experience with Edge AI or deploying algorithms in resource-constrained environments (IoT, Aerospace, or Mobile).
  • Direct experience with the Defense and Intelligence community, particularly in GEOINT or tactical ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance).
  • Current US Security Clearance or the ability to obtain and maintain one.

title: Senior Satellite Constellation Manager
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:  

The Senior Satellite Constellation (CxS) Manager is responsible for steering initiatives that will deliver on Planet’s growing CxS business line throughout the EMEA market. This role will be responsible for programs valued over €100M to build, launch and operate a constellation of Pelican satellites for Planet and its strategic EMEA customers. This is a key strategic role for the Space Business Group and Planet as a whole. The Senior Manager will achieve success by coordinating teams and resources across the company to ensure that the capabilities delivered by the program meet customer requirements and provide excellent imagery products. The Senior Manager must ensure that the Pelicans delivered on orbit are healthy, productive satellites and will hire or enlist teammates that will establish plans, programs, and teams that maximize the chance for mission success. They will coordinate with Mission Directors and peers in Space Systems, Software Engineering, and other corporate functions to deconflict resources and balance timelines so Planet can maximize the overall success of its mission portfolio.

The Senior Manager is successful through establishing a core program team to drive the top-level mission elements while enabling and empowering the executing teams. Thus, they must demonstrate a strong commitment to Planet’s cultural values, agile principles, and servant leadership to succeed in this role. They enhance their effectiveness through generating personal credibility and building relationships across the company.

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our Berlin office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Acts as the Directly Responsible Individual (DRI) on overall program success, ensuring that the capabilities delivered by the program meet the needs of the customer and serve the customer and Planet’s business model.
  • Serves as the primary regional "bridge," ensuring that local EMEA European mission nuances and market developments are seamlessly integrated into Planet’s global strategic vision.
  • Accountable for ensuring that the program maintains close alignment to Pelicans commercial, technical and product roadmap, that it complements Planet’s other High Resolution and Constellation Service product offerings
  • Represents the program internally to senior leadership and the broader company. 
  • You will work with Space Business Group leadership to make sure that the program is best positioned and supported to achieve its objectives.
  • Lead, manage, and hire the core program team. This core team has scope that reaches across divisional and organizational boundaries and has the mandate to lead the program through integrated execution.
  • Develops and maintains high level cross-company program priorities and processes while addressing blockers to progress.
  • Holds overall program risk and balances risk across the various program elements, including engineering, launch, and operations.
  • Acts as the accountable individual for satisfying program-level requirements. If requirements need to change the Mission Director is Planet’s facilitator and negotiator of such changes between the customer and Planet. 
  • Supports Systems Engineering in coordinating internal requirement flow among Product/Sales and the engineering divisions.
  • Works with the EPM (Engineering Program Manager) and TPM (Technical Product Manager) teams to maintain a clear view of program schedule and program-level risks to guide program strategy and execution.
  • Collaborates with finance and accounting teams to develop and apply standard practices for program financial management.
  • Acts as Planet’s ambassador for the program to potential partners, customers and the press as needed.

What You Bring:

  • 10+ years experience in space systems development, management and/or operations
  • Deep expertise in LEO satellite operations, satellite design, and what is required to deliver mission success.
  • Generally knowledgeable about tasking business models and remote sensing technology.
  • The ability to operate effectively and decisively in an information rich environment with minimal guidance and extended autonomy.
  • The ability to make decisions that align with Planet’s technical and strategic roadmap as well as Planet’s core values.
  • Willingness to hold ambiguity and risk for the organization, and to make decisions when needed to bring clarity to executing teams.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills where team alignment can consistently be delivered and maintained.
  • The ability to travel within Europe as needed and to California 2-3 times per year. 
  • Ability to maintain high availability during the start of the California workday (Pacific Time) for scheduled meetings and sporadic real-time collaboration.

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Demonstrated success in developing, managing, or operating large satellite constellations
  • Expertise in leading and delivering major corporate initiatives through leveraging cross-departmental teams
  • Examples of proactively managing and mitigating program risk for space programs

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

The team responsible for Identity and Access Management (IAM) in the Planet Insights Platform is looking for a Senior Software Engineer, passionate about building a great platform that other teams and customers want to use. Our team mission is continuous evolution of the IAM services of the platform, enabling wide adoption of the platform as the foundation for building new solutions in the Earth observation space both for internal and external customers. Our current activities are focused on facilitating M2M communication between all platform services and making our services the enabler of secure and controlled data access in rapid, AI powered solution development.

Your job will be maintaining and evolving our IAM services and web applications with a focus on making the platform secure, reliable and easy to use. This includes working with IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service). The IAM team is using React for the front-end, and Java for the back end. We take a modern software development approach and follow and evaluate new developments in the field.

This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our Ljubljana office 3 days per week.

Impact You’ll Own:

  • Evolve existing IAM services and develop new features.
  • Design the future IAM services in collaboration with multiple teams and cross organizational stakeholders.
  • Champion the platform adoption, developing tools and libraries and writing technical documentation to support engineering teams to integrate with IAM. 
  • Help maintain web applications owned by the team. 
  • Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues related to IAM solutions.
  • Mentor junior colleagues.
  • Conduct design and code reviews.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of experience in software development.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.
  • Cross-functional collaboration skills.
  • Ability to lead and drive domain driven and technical discussions with several stakeholders, balance their needs and arrive at a working solution.
  • Ability to technically lead and set example to a team of developers.
  • Experience with Java or other similar object oriented language.
  • Experience with REST APIs, HTTP(S).
  • Good grasp of relational databases.
  • Professional working proficiency in English, the language of the company.

What Makes You Stand Out:

  • Experience with React and front-end development.
  • Experience with Python and/or Go.
  • Experience in authentication technologies such as OAuth and IAM.
  • Experience building backend services and infrastructure that scales and is easy to evolve; GCP or AWS.
  • Expertise with Java 21+ for the backend; JAX-RS, CDI, Jackson.
  • Experience with the Grafana ecosystem.
  • Positive attitude, excitement about Planet’s mission.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description_text:
Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

The defense and intel pillar is looking for a full-stack software engineer who will be responsible for building systems and integrating with our federal customers. In this role, you will work with multiple internal teams to build our next generation global monitoring application that will drive our next decade of growth.

Your work will be highly visible and will directly impact the ability of customers to get faster access to Planet’s unprecedented datasets. The ideal candidate would have full-stack experience, with experience developing backend services, frontend applications, and data solutions.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a relevant field

About the Role:

Planet is looking for a Senior Engineering Manager with a proven background in guiding and mentoring high-performing engineering teams.

In this role, you will be responsible for the Defense Intelligence Integration engineering team. The mission of this team is to bring Planet’s catalog of imagery and tasking to large Defense & Intelligence customers. Your work will be highly visible and will directly impact the ability of customers to get faster access to Planet’s unprecedented datasets

This is very much a “servant leadership” role, with a high emphasis on enabling your team members to do their best work in an environment that sets them up for success. In particular, you’ll ensure engineers are unblocked on dependencies, take the lead in defining the team’s work, and organize that work in a way that best aligns with organizational and team practices. The team has a highly collaborative culture and is distributed remotely across the Earth.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:

  • Guide your team of software engineers and data scientists to deliver successful products for the Global Monitoring Service.
  • Mentor, coach and unblock your team members, helping them succeed and grow their careers.
  • Ensure efficient shipping of product improvements and features.
  • Partner with your product management counterpart to define strategy and take tactical decisions.
  • Refine and implement team processes, including for task planning, code reviews and on-call rotations.
  • Establish sound engineering practices to build software and design training & inference pipelines.
  • Collaborate across our engineering organization and other cross functional teams to align efforts, particularly concerning model maintenance, deployment pipelines, and new compute infrastructure needs.

What You Bring:

  • 6+ years of related experience.
  • 4+ years of experience as a manager, with ability to build, retain, and grow diverse, geographically dispersed teams.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to explain complex technical issues accurately to technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Experience in delivering data-intensive software products at scale.
  • Solid understanding of fundamentals in software engineering, statistics and machine learning.
  • Success in establishing and evolving operational practices in a software engineering organization.
  • Experience with  change management, managing tech debt, and analyzing tradeoffs in solution builds.
  • Experience with distributed cloud computing.

What Makes You Stand Out: 

  • Experience building products using sensor or geospatial data.
  • Experience working with satellite or airborne earth observations data.
  • Interest in defense, intelligence or spatial imagery.
